skill.name: parametric-scribe
skill.description: Records AI coding tasks in a recipe file and replays or forks prior steps so later work can adapt to changed decisions.
skill.summary: Track, replay, and fork AI coding history with a recipe-based workflow.
skill.author: Cycleaddict

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-cryptography matches, but most are false positives from Markdown backticks, YAML examples, and prose. One real concern remains: the replay workflow instructs the assistant to run a git checkout against a stored SHA, which can alter the working tree and should require user confirmation.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Git Checkout Instruction
medium_findings.description: Replay mode instructs the assistant to run git checkout against a prior step SHA. This is a legitimate version-control workflow, but it can change the working tree and may discard uncommitted work if used without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly shown in the replay protocol, and its operational effect is clear. It is not evidence of malware, but it is a real destructive workflow risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Mostly False Positive
low_findings.description: The static command-execution alerts mostly point to inline Markdown code, file names, or a fenced YAML recipe example. These are documentation patterns rather than executable code, except for the git checkout instruction tracked separately.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible documentation or YAML examples, not executable source files. Manual review found no script body, shell wrapper, or hidden command runner.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts point to descriptive text, not a cryptographic algorithm or hash implementation. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, or other weak cryptographic use in the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain natural-language descriptions of the skill. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm name, or security-sensitive hash logic at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Version-Control State Change Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The replay protocol includes git checkout of a prior SHA. Assistants should confirm a clean working tree and explicit user approval before executing this command.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented and has a clear state-changing effect. Risk depends on execution context and whether user confirmation is enforced.

content.value_statement: AI coding sessions can lose context when earlier decisions change. Parametric Scribe records each task as a recipe step so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can replay and adapt later work.
actual_capabilities: Defines a docs/recipe.yaml format for recording coding steps, prompts, files, and git SHAs.
Guides assistants to append a recipe entry after each coding task.
Describes replay from a selected step using the original intent of later steps.
Supports creating variant recipe files for alternative project paths.
Includes an example recipe.yaml file with recorded project steps.
limitations: Does not include executable tooling or automated replay scripts.
Replay quality depends on the assistant interpreting prior prompts correctly.
Git checkout operations can alter local files and need user confirmation.
The recipe must be maintained consistently to remain useful.

use_cases.title: Change an Early Technical Decision
use_cases.description: Replace a framework or data layer choice and ask the assistant to replay later features against the new foundation.

use_cases.title: Review AI Development History
use_cases.description: Inspect recorded prompts, modified files, and step intent to understand how a project evolved over time.

use_cases.title: Fork Product Variants
use_cases.description: Create a separate recipe variant for an alternate direction while preserving the original implementation path.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Recipe
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin recording a new AI coding session.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Parametric Scribe for this project. Create docs/recipe.yaml and record each completed task with intent, prompt, changed files, and git SHA.

prompt_templates.title: Record Current Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Add a completed task to the project recipe.
prompt_templates.prompt: Append a new recipe step for the task we just completed. Include the intent, exact prompt, modified files, timestamp, and current git SHA.

prompt_templates.title: Replay From a Step
prompt_templates.scenario: Change an earlier implementation choice and adapt later work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Change Step 2 to use PostgreSQL instead of SQLite. Confirm before any git checkout, then replay later steps using their original intent.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Variant
prompt_templates.scenario: Explore an alternate project path without replacing the original recipe.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a recipe variant for a FastAPI implementation. Preserve the original recipe, change the target step, and describe how later steps should adapt.

output_examples.input: Record the authentication work we just completed.
output: Added a new recipe step with the authentication intent.
Captured the original prompt, changed files, timestamp, and current git SHA.
Noted that future replay should preserve the login and session behavior.

output_examples.input: Change Step 1 from Flask to Django and replay later steps.
output: Reviewed the recipe and identified the affected steps.
Requested confirmation before changing the git working tree.
Adapted the later authentication and routing steps to Django conventions.
Updated the recipe with new files and replay notes.

output_examples.input: Create a variant that uses FastAPI.
output: Created a separate variant recipe for the FastAPI path.
Listed the steps that need adaptation for the alternate framework.

best_practices: Confirm a clean git working tree before any replay or checkout operation.
Write clear task intent so later replay can adapt behavior instead of copying files blindly.
Review recipe changes after each task to keep prompts, files, and SHAs accurate.
anti_patterns: Running replay commands without explicit user approval can overwrite local work.
Recording vague prompts makes later adaptation unreliable.
Editing project files outside the recipe workflow weakens the recorded history.

faq.question: Does this skill run code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation-based workflow that guides an assistant to maintain and use a recipe file.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark medium risk?
faq.answer: The replay protocol includes a git checkout instruction that can change local files. Users should approve that action first.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is written for AI coding assistants and is compatible with those supported tools.

faq.question: Where is the project history stored?
faq.answer: The skill stores history in docs/recipe.yaml and can create variant recipe files for alternate paths.

faq.question: What makes replay work?
faq.answer: Replay uses each step intent and prompt to adapt later work to the current codebase.

faq.question: What should I check before replaying history?
faq.answer: Check git status, commit or save important work, and confirm the exact step to replay from.
